input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to benchmark three methods (AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, UniqueWordCount) from the TextAnalyzer project, comparing .NET 9 vs .NET 10 performance. Let me start by exploring the codebase and checking what .NET versions are available.

data.content: Now I'll create a BenchmarkDotNet project that multi-targets net9.0 and net10.0.
data.reasoningText: Now I need to create a BenchmarkDotNet project that benchmarks the three methods (AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, UniqueWordCount) targeting both net9.0 and net10.0. I'll create a benchmark project that multi-targets both frameworks.

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-b84637d4fa5947438c6d36ce8b228f30/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj b/tmp/sv-b84637d4fa5947438c6d36ce8b228f30/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-b84637d4fa5947438c6d36ce8b228f30/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.*" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

data.reasoningText: Wait, actually the Benchmarks project includes the TextAnalyzer.cs file directly via `<Compile Include>`, so it doesn't reference the TextAnalyzer project. The TextAnalyzer.csproj change was unnecessary but shouldn't cause issues. Let me run the benchmark now.

Actually, let me just try using a manual Job definition instead, or use the `[SimpleJob]` attribute with the runtime specified differently. Or better yet, let me just use `--runtimes` CLI argument or configure jobs manually.

Actually, the simplest approach since we're multi-targeting: just don't use `SimpleJob` attributes with RuntimeMoniker. 

I could pass the runtimes directly via command line arguments like `--runtimes net9.0 net10.0`, or define the jobs manually with `Job.Default` and specify the runtime configuration that way.
